headline: Why Workday (WDAY) Shares Are Sliding Today
summary: Shares of enterprise software company Workday (NASDAQ:WDAY) fell 2.5% in the morning session after two Wall Street firms issued bearish calls, highlighted by Morgan Stanley downgrading the stock from Equal Weight to Underweight. The firm slashed its price target from $185 to $145. Analysts argued that the pace of AI-driven growth would be gradual, creating an uncertain growth and margin outlook. headline: OneSource Virtual Launches Federally Chartered Trust Bank
summary: DALLAS, July 21, 2026--OneSource Virtual (OSV), a leading provider of HR and payments services for the Workday ecosystem, today announced the launch of OneSource Virtual Trust Bank, N.A. (OSVTB), a federally chartered national trust bank regulated by the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC). The charter, which took effect July 1, 2026, capped a multi-year effort to build a dedicated banking entity for the funds OSV holds and manages on behalf of its customers.

headline: Adobe, Salesforce and Intuit fall as Morgan Stanley begins cautious coverage
summary: Investing.com -- Morgan Stanley took a mixed stance on software stocks Tuesday, growing bearish on Adobe and Workday even as it maintained an ’Attractive’ view on the sector overall.

headline: Chip Stocks Surge but Software Sector Feels the Pain Again
summary: Technology stocks were surging early Tuesday but the beaten-down software sector was only enduring more pain as the artificial-intelligence trade sparked back into life. Chip stocks, including Intel, Micron and Marvell were among the biggest risers ahead of the open, along with memory storage names Sandisk and Western Digital.
